channel.title: Delta V IntelHub — All Signals
channel.description: Curated intelligence feed from Delta V

item.title: Google Is Suing Chinese Scammers Who Are Using Gemini
item.description: pNot sure a href="https://arstechnica.com/google/2026/06/google-sues-chinese-cybercrime-network-that-used-gemini-to-automate-scams/"this/a will have any effect, but I support the effort:/pblockquotepAccording to Google&#8217;s legal filing, Outsider Enterprise operates through Telegram. The group of

item.title: A conceptor by any other name
item.description: pspanI just had one of those delightful moments where I have a very specific idea, and then I search for it (Claude Research in this case), and it turns out that people have already been using that /spanbspanexact/span/bspan concept but just calling it by a name I'd never heard of./span/ppspanThat n

item.title: Four Bitcoin Bottoms, One Signal: the 100-Day NUPL Below Zero “Either the 100-day EMA crosses zero as it did at every prior bottom, or this becomes the first cycle to bottom without it, which would fi
item.description: pFour Bitcoin Bottoms, One Signal: the 100-Day NUPL Below Zerobr /br /“Either the 100-day EMA crosses zero as it did at every prior bottom, or this becomes the first cycle to bottom without it, which would fit the shallower-each-time trend.” – By a href="https://nitter.net/thechessONCHAIN" title="ch

item.title: Bots constantly flooding with malware on GitHub, same stars count on the new repositories, and GitHub's process is too slow / time consuming to report all.

item.title: A complete bug bounty workspace for HackerOne researchers. Includes scope enforcement, automated recon/vuln pipeline (400+ tools), report templates, CVE/CWE watchlists, and a local VM practice lab. Bu
item.description: pA complete bug bounty workspace for HackerOne researchers. Includes scope enforcement, automated recon/vuln pipeline (400+ tools), report templates, CVE/CWE watchlists, and a local VM practice lab. Built for disciplined, ethical hunting. a href="https://github.com/DevCop95/bugbounty-lab101"github.c

item.title: MiCA. DORA. Evolving regulator expectations across major markets. They're all converging on the same baseline: continuous monitoring, real-time risk controls, demonstrable audit trails. Institutions b
item.description: pMiCA. DORA. Evolving regulator expectations across major markets.br /br /They're all converging on the same baseline: continuous monitoring, real-time risk controls, demonstrable audit trails.br /br /Institutions building this into their infrastructure now won't have to rebuild from scratch when th

item.title: Someone opened a 10x short on 3,753.56 $SNDK ($6.27M) on @Aster_DEX and is now up $116K (+18.53%). https://www.asterdex.com/en/explorer/address/0xb84BEBA977D309eC9D44ac45e78817Ca009ef890

item.title: Pinned: LeRobot v0.6.0 is officially here: Imagine, Evaluate, Improve! 🤖🚀 We are closing the robot learning loop with massive upgrades for the open-source robotics community. From policies that imagin
item.description: pLeRobot v0.6.0 is officially here: Imagine, Evaluate, Improve! 🤖🚀br /br /We are closing the robot learning loop with massive upgrades for the open-source robotics community. From policies that imagine the future to a much leaner installation, here is what is new:br /- 🌍 World Models: VLA-JEPA, Ling

item.title: Two memcached heap buffer overflows — default-config LRU-crawler metadump + proxy backend-response length truncation (→ RCE). Found with Raptor https://github.com/dinosn/memcached-security-2026
item.description: pTwo memcached heap buffer overflows — default-config LRU-crawler metadump + proxy backend-response length truncation (→ RCE). Found with Raptor a href="https://github.com/dinosn/memcached-security-2026"github.com/dinosn/memcached-…/a/phr /bLink/bbr /a href="https://github.com/dinosn/memcached-secu

item.title: New release on memcached, resolving the 2 RCE cases found yesterday. Awesome work from the team, from my initial email to resolution just in couple hours. Thank you. Needless to say, update to the lat
item.description: pNew release on memcached, resolving the 2 RCE cases found yesterday. Awesome work from the team, from my initial email to resolution just in couple hours. Thank you. Needless to say, update to the latest version, one case is a direct RCE. (I will put them on blog post at some point in time) a hre

item.title: Join Our AI Safety × Philosophy Reading Group
item.description: pspanWe believe that ensuring AI goes well does not only require technical skills. Every technical choice rests on philosophical assumptions that technical training does not prepare you to examine. If those assumptions are wrong, then technical excellence alone is not enough./span/ppspanBecause of t

item.title: The Geometry of Yes: Mapping Sycophancy Inside an LLM's Emotion Space
item.description: h1spanSummary/span/h1pspanLLMs have internal emotion representations that causally shape their behaviour. This was recently observed in Claude: positive emotions like happy and loving are linked to sycophancy, and you can steer the model by directly manipulating these directions in activation space.

item.title: Another Look at 'Slack'
item.description: pa href="https://thezvi.substack.com/p/slack"spanSlack/span/aspan by Zvi is a much-loved post which captures something important. But the core idea’s contours can be hard to pin down, and people attempting to get a tighter grip haven’t converged on a better characterisation. Slack is the /spana href

item.title: Ranges of Probabilities: What Are They For?
item.description: pspanConsider the following dialogue, between two forecasters aiming to predict the next presidential election:/span/pblockquotepspanForecaster Alice: I believe that candidate X will win the election, with probability 36.5%./span/ppspanForecaster Bob: I believe that candidate X will win the election

item.title: Architecture matters for multi-agent security
item.description: pispanA summary of our ICML 2026 paper,/span/ia href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.23459"ispan Architecture Matters for Multi-Agent Security/span/i/aispan (/span/ia href="https://benhagag.com/" rel="noreferrer"ispanBen Hagag,/span/i/aispan /span/ia href="https://wlanderson.com/" rel="noreferrer"ispanW

item.title: AI Safety Can't Afford a Second Cause
item.description: pspanImagine an astronomer who discovers an asteroid with a 50% chance of hitting Earth in 2035. She goes on TV. She testifies before Congress. She founds the Asteroid Deflection Institute and starts doing fundraising rounds. And then, in between appearances, she prolifically tweets about zoning ref

item.title: Data filtering works a lot worse than you would expect
item.description: pispanThis work was largely done during Neel Nanda's MATS 10.0 Exploration Phase. /span/i/ppispanJ Rosser and Dohun Lee are co-first authors for this post with equal contribution. Josh Engels and Neel Nanda supervised the project, and provided guidance and feedback throughout. /span/i/ph1bspanTLDR/

item.title: Data filtering works a lot worse than you would expect
item.description: pispanThis work was largely done during Neel Nanda's MATS 10.0 Exploration Phase. /span/i/ppispanJ Rosser and Dohun Lee are co-first authors for this post with equal contribution. item.title: When does a chess transformer “see” a knight fork? An initial result from logit lens and attention patterns
item.description: pspan(parts 2 and 3 to follow)/span/ph1bspanSummary of this post/span/b/h1pspanThis post is on the results of a mechanistic interpretability project aimed at understanding the internals of Maia 3: a transformer based chess bot trained to imitate human play at a chosen skill level, rather than to pla
